message:
feat(tailer): Add TailerSupervisor for adaptive pane polling

Implement the tailer supervisor for managing per-pane content capture
with adaptive polling intervals. This optimizes resource usage by
polling active panes more frequently than idle ones.

TailerConfig:
- min_interval/max_interval: Bounds for adaptive polling (50ms - 1s default)
- backoff_multiplier: Rate of slowdown for idle panes (1.5x default)
- max_concurrent: Limit concurrent captures to avoid overload
- overlap_size: Delta extraction overlap for continuity detection

PaneTailer (internal):
- Tracks per-pane polling state with current interval
- Adaptive interval adjustment: snaps to min on activity, backs off
  exponentially (capped at max) on idle

TailerSupervisor:
- sync_tailers(): Add/remove tailers to match observed pane set
- poll_once(): Capture from panes ready for polling (respects max_concurrent)
- shutdown(): Graceful cleanup of all tailer state
- Metrics tracking: events sent, timeouts, no-change captures

CaptureEvent wrapper for CapturedSegment to enable persistence pipeline.

Note: Current implementation is a placeholder - actual WezTerm CLI
integration for content fetching is deferred to future work.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
